methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $175M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Axiom Space, a Series G space infrastructure company founded by Michael T. Suffredini and Kam Ghaffarian, has raised $350M to date. The valuation exercise operates within a Space & Aerospace sector universe comprising 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX and Sierra Space, though narrow-stage peer data remains constrained.

Stage-progression analysis was the sole applicable triangulation method. Using total capital raised ($350M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.25x Series G step-up multiplier yields an implied value of $219M. This method reflects typical dilution and risk reduction between funding rounds but carries meaningful uncertainty given the absence of comparable same-stage or adjacent-stage transactions in the dataset. Public-comp anchoring (sector P/S median: 25.8x) cannot be deployed without disclosed revenue. Peer-median analysis was not feasible due to insufficient datapoints meeting stage and tier criteria across the 11 same-sector peers identified.

The triangulated range spans $186M (low) to $252M (high), with a median of $219M. This narrow one-method result reflects methodological constraint rather than conviction. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, contract backlog specificity, and the absence of recent comparable private transactions in commercial space infrastructure at this stage. Additional disclosure of near-term customer commitments or revenue run-rate would materially sharpen the valuation bandwidth.
